element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player locked into 6-class choice (Fighter, Thief, Black Belt, Red Mage, White Mage, Black Mage) for full playthrough
Travel by foot initially; ship, canoe, airship unlock progressively
Enemies on foot/canoe/ship travel; mandatory encounter or flee decision
Citizens provide information; shops sell items/equipment; narrative advancement
Forests, caves, mountains, swamps, underwater caverns, buildings; treasure chests with rare items
Attack, Magic, Item options; turn-based; flee available
Levels 1-50; increases HP and attributes; gained through combat wins

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available per class (Red Mage, White Mage, Black Mage from start; others via class upgrade)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Unlocked through levels and class progression
Character sprite maturation; new weapon/magic access for upgraded classes
Four Elemental Fiends darken crystals; preset boss locations; crystal restoration objective

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Grinding-based progression with grinding fatigue as documented criticism; tension cycles through random encounters interspersed with safe town/shop periods; boss gauntlet structure forces resource management

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Treasure chests in dungeons signal optional rare items not in shops

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foot-based overworld travel; basic random encounters; introductory dungeons with low-tier enemies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Ship/canoe unlock; dungeon complexity increases; class upgrades available; Elemental Fiend encounters intensify
difficulty_curve.late_game: Airship unlocked; access to end-game dungeons; final Elemental Fiends; preset boss encounters
notable_spikes: Elemental Fiend boss battles (story-gated preset locations)
Late-game dungeon gauntlets requiring sustained preparation

notable_level_patterns.description: Story progresses by alternating safe town exploration (information, shops, healing) with dangerous dungeon crawling; creates rhythm of resource management and relief cycles
Overworld accessibility progressively expands as ship (early), canoe (mid), and airship (late) become available; each transport type gates new regions and dungeons

Four Elemental Fiends located in distinct dungeons (Earth, Fire, Water, Wind fiends implied by crystal darkening plot); mandatory boss encounters at dungeon endpoints; fiend defeat restores crystal light
Open-world random encounters used for level grinding and resource depletion; bosses and story events in preset dungeon locations provide narrative checkpoints and difficulty spikes
